ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographic region of the country */,
  "data_collection_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of data collection (e.g., annually, biennially) */,
  "data_collection_method" TEXT /* Method used to collect data (e.g., surveys, national health records) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the data belongs to */,
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "total_litres_of_pure_alcohol" REAL /* Total liters of pure alcohol consumed per capita */,
  "beer_servings" INTEGER /* Average number of beer servings per person per day */,
  "spirit_servings" INTEGER /* Average number of spirit servings per person per day */,
  "wine_servings" INTEGER /* Average number of wine servings per person per day */,
  "data_collection_year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "data_accuracy" TEXT /* Accuracy or reliability of the data */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date the dataset was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_datasets_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_datasets_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the source */,
  "data_source_type" TEXT /* Type of data source (e.g., government, NGO, research institute) */,
  "data_source_url" TEXT /* URL to the data source for reference */,
  "data_source_contact" TEXT /* Contact information for the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the type */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the unit of measurement */,
  "data_type_category" TEXT /* Category of the data type (e.g., consumption, production) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_units" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each unit */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name of the unit */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the unit */,
  "unit_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the unit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dataset_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the version belongs to */,
  "version_name" TEXT /* Name of the version */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the version was released */,
  "version_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the version changes */,
  "version_status" TEXT /* Status of the version (e.g., active, deprecated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dataset_versions_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the update belongs to */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date the update was made */,
  "update_description" TEXT /* Description of the update */,
  "update_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the update */,
  "update_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_updates_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the dataset */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the dataset was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user the permission belongs to */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the permission belongs to */,
  "permission_type" TEXT /* Type of permission (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  "permission_scope" TEXT /* Scope of the permission (e.g., country, global) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "role_permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-role assignment */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user the role belongs to */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "role_start_date" TEXT /* Date the user was assigned the role */,
  "role_end_date" TEXT /* Date the role assignment ends */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dataset_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the category belongs to */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "category_description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dataset_categories_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dataset_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the tag belongs to */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  "tag_description" TEXT /* Description of the tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dataset_tags_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_visualizations" (
  "visualization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each visualization */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the visualization belongs to */,
  "visualization_type" TEXT /* Type of visualization (e.g., chart, graph) */,
  "visualization_data" TEXT /* Data for the visualization */,
  "visualization_title" TEXT /* Title of the visualization */,
  "visualization_description" TEXT /* Description of the visualization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("visualization_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_visualizations_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the report belongs to */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Data for the report */,
  "report_summary" TEXT /* Summary of the report */,
  "report_published" TEXT /* Date the report was published */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)
